description_long: This is a small, traditional Japanese Shinto shrine constructed primarily from dark wood, elevated on a stone base. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with gray tiles, featuring prominent crossed wooden finials (chigi) and short horizontal billets (katsuogi) at the ridge, which are characteristic of Shinto architecture. The front of the shrine displays a decorative rope (shimenawa) with paper streamers, a small offering box, and a wooden plaque with Japanese writing. The structure is compact, with solid wooden walls and a slightly projecting roof eave supported by brackets. A separate wooden signpost stands adjacent to the shrine, likely providing information or prayers.
description: A compact, dark wooden Shinto shrine with a steep tiled roof, crossed finials, and a decorative rope above an offering box, set on a stone base with an adjacent signpost.
description_view_0: Front view showing the entry with shimenawa rope, offering box, and the adjacent wooden signpost.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tiled roof, wooden structure, and the signpost's profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wooden back and the crossed roof finials.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the roof tiles and the back of the signpost.
attribute_entryway_design: rectangular wooden entry with shimenawa rope and offering box
